page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The instance is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data positions this instance in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core Geekbench score of 1368.0 and a multi-core score of 7008.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 14790.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 635.15 GB/sec, which drops to 43.56 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. In database operations, the instance handles 11.32 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 5645.05 on Passmark database tests.

This hardware profile is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 9.87 GB/sec, as well as software compilation and text processing. However, it exhibits performance limitations for complex machine learning tasks. While it processes a small 135M parameter LLM model at 1041.9 tokens/sec for prompts, its performance drops to a weak tier for medium 7B models, yielding only 7.28 tokens/sec for text generation. This makes the instance highly cost-efficient for standard enterprise applications, utility servers, and moderate database tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark results place this server in the average tier for standard compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7008.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 11.32 million operations per second. While highly capable for static web serving, software compilation, and standard database operations, it exhibits weak performance on larger LLM inference tasks. This makes the instance a cost-efficient choice for general-purpose enterprise workloads, web serving, and moderate application hosting.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.3xlarge is a general-purpose KVM instance with 12 Intel Xeon cores, 48 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.3xlarge features 12 Intel Xeon cores, 48 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps bandwidth, ideal for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring twelve dedicated cores and forty-eight gigabytes of memory for general-purpose enterprise workloads.

48.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average performance in standard database and web serving benchmarks
Suited for software compilation, web hosting, and general-purpose applications
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
